title: Origami Folds Hide Conductive Paths In Plain Sight
excerpt: Create electrical traces by just folding a paperlike material
content_html:
What could you do if you could make a circuit trace by just bending a piece of paper? How about bridging modern technologies and traditional handicrafts while providing opportunities for learning skills in both.

As part of our interdisciplinary research into digital craftsmanship at the MEI Lab at the School of Creative Media, City University of Hong Kong, we came across research that demonstrated how to impregnate paperlike material (technically a “nonwoven textile”) with the kind of liquid metal used to make conductive ink. Initially, the impregnated material is nonconductive because an insulating oxide layer forms that encapsulates microscopic droplets of the liquid metal. However, applying pressure via shaped molds will crack open the insulating layer, allowing neighboring particles to merge, and thus creating conducting regions in the shape of the mold.

Both of us were introduced as children to origami and kirigami (similar to origami, except that cutting is allowed in addition to folding). We, along with our colleagues, decided to see if those traditional techniques could be used on the new material to eliminate the need for molds. Our goal was to allow crafters to make hybrid papercraft creations that contained easily integrated elements such as LEDs and motors.

In particular, we were interested in the possibility of combining the separate stages of creating a papercraft object and adding electrical conductors. Previous approaches to creating electrified papercraft objects relied on adding a separate flexible conductor—such as adhesive copper tape—to the paper. This increases the effort required and runs the risk of creating open circuits as the conductive material conforms to the object’s shape.

Isopropanol and a gallium-indium liquid material are used to impregnate a paperlike material that is 55 percent polyester and 45 percent cellulose. Electronic components such as LEDs and motors are held in place with masking tape. James Provost

Our first step was to see if the pressures involved in bending and cutting alone would be sufficient to create conductive traces. We became frequent visitors to our university’s materials science and engineering department to fabricate samples and then to borrow equipment to characterize their behavior.

We soon confirmed that the pressures involved in folding and cutting—ranging from 2.5 to 100 megapascals—were enough to create conductive traces. We also confirmed that normal handling of the paper didn’t accidentally create conductive paths.

We made a number of changes to the original method for creating the impregnated paper. For example, instead of immersing the paper in a mixture of isopropanol and liquid metal, we used an airbrush to spray the mixture onto the paper. That allowed us to vary how much was deposited on the paper and to use cardboard stencils to mask some areas from being impregnated, allowing folding and cutting in those regions without creating unwanted conductive traces. We also experimented with the ratios of isopropanol and liquid metal.

We became frequent visitors to our university’s materials science and engineering department.

After optimizing the mixing ratios and amount applied via airbrush, we were left with a material that reliably conducts with a resistance of 23.18 ohms per centimeter for cut edges and 4.4 Ω/cm for folded edges. The folded edges retain their conductivity even if later flattened out, and the conductivity is the same on either side of the paper. We estimate the combined cost of the paper and liquid metal (available from many online vendors) is about US $1.80 to make a 10- by 10-cm piece.

The next step was attaching electronic components to the traces. To make the connections more flexible, we cut down the rigid leads of LEDs and attached conductive thread to the stumps. We then held the threads in place using masking tape. Similarly, we connected conductive thread to the terminals of a power supply.

As our goal was to use this material educationally, we now needed to make it easy for a beginner—whether in papercraft or electronics—to try it out. We created a toolkit, dubbed LiqMetCraft. This consists of all the required materials, plus a browser-based software tool that lets the user select or create designs and then gives guidance on physical construction.

We created three versions of LiqMetCraft. The first is based on Chinese papercraft in which a piece of paper is folded into a fanlike segment and then cut to create a radially symmetric design. We provided circles of paper with a doughnot-shape impregnated region, with an untreated region that created a gap in the donut. We attached positive and negative terminals to either side of the gap. The user could specify in the software how many times they wanted to fold the disk and then draw potential cuts, receiving immediate feedback on what the unfolded disk would look like, as well as guidance on how to place LEDs.

To make our paper sample, isopropanol and liquid metal are mixed in specific ratios while being cooled by an ice bath. Sonic waves are used to ensure the liquid metal breaks up into microscopic droplets. The mixture is then applied via airbrush, while stencils prevent some areas being covered for different papercraft templates. James Provost

The second version of LiqMetCraft was based on origami. We supplied rectangular pieces of paper with two conductive regions separated by a border down the middle. The software tool provided templates for 12 origami designs, with step-by-step instructions for folding them. Once the project was completed, the user could add components, such as a motor, by taping them to the folds.

The final version supported 3D paper model making. In this case, the initial paper supplied was a rectangle with an untreated rectangular central area. By cutting this paper in half and then further cutting the halves into patterns separated by a spacer, the user could make various self-standing models. The software allowed the user to draw a pattern on screen, and then have a cutting machine produce a template for cutting the impregnated paper.

We had 42 participants, evenly divided into three groups, try out the different versions. All found it easy to use, and we were pleasantly surprised that some participants moved beyond the supplied designs to their own creations.

For full details of the current process, see our open access LiqMetCraft research paper published in CHI ‘26: Proceedings of the 2026 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems. In the future, we plan to try different substrates for the impregnating solution, as well as explore further types of papercraft, such as pop-up books. We’re also interested in developing ways to use the material to support inputs as well as outputs by constructing switches and potentiometers directly out of the material. Imagine traditional papercraft creations becoming interactive devices!

This article appears in the July 2026 print issue.

blocks.html_en: What could you do if you could make a circuit trace by just bending a piece of paper? How about bridging modern technologies and traditional handicrafts while providing opportunities for learning skills in both.

sentences.en: What could you do if you could make a circuit trace by just bending a piece of paper?
sentences.zh: 如果只需弯曲一张纸就能做出电路走线，你会做什么？

sentences.en: How about bridging modern technologies and traditional handicrafts while providing opportunities for learning skills in both.
sentences.zh: 如何将现代技术和传统手工艺联系起来，同时提供学习这两种技能的机会？

blocks.html_en: As part of our interdisciplinary research into digital craftsmanship at the MEI Lab at the School of Creative Media, City University of Hong Kong, we came across research that demonstrated how to impregnate paperlike material (technically a “nonwoven textile”) with the kind of liquid metal used to make conductive ink. Initially, the impregnated material is nonconductive because an insulating oxide layer forms that encapsulates microscopic droplets of the liquid metal. However, applying pressure via shaped molds will crack open the insulating layer, allowing neighboring particles to merge, and thus creating conducting regions in the shape of the mold.

sentences.en: As part of our interdisciplinary research into digital craftsmanship at the MEI Lab at the School of Creative Media, City University of Hong Kong, we came across research that demonstrated how to impregnate paperlike material (technically a “nonwoven textile”) with the kind of liquid metal used to make conductive ink.
sentences.zh: 作为香港城市大学创意媒体学院 MEI 实验室数字工艺跨学科研究的一部分，我们发现了一项研究，展示了如何用用于制造导电墨水的液态金属浸渍纸状材料（技术上是“无纺布”）。

sentences.en: Initially, the impregnated material is nonconductive because an insulating oxide layer forms that encapsulates microscopic droplets of the liquid metal.
sentences.zh: 最初，浸渍材料是不导电的，因为形成了绝缘氧化物层，封装了液态金属的微小液滴。

sentences.en: However, applying pressure via shaped molds will crack open the insulating layer, allowing neighboring particles to merge, and thus creating conducting regions in the shape of the mold.
sentences.zh: 然而，通过成型模具施加压力会使绝缘层破裂，使相邻颗粒合并，从而在模具形状中形成导电区域。

blocks.html_en: Both of us were introduced as children to origami and kirigami (similar to origami, except that cutting is allowed in addition to folding). We, along with our colleagues, decided to see if those traditional techniques could be used on the new material to eliminate the need for molds. Our goal was to allow crafters to make hybrid papercraft creations that contained easily integrated elements such as LEDs and motors.

sentences.en: Both of us were introduced as children to origami and kirigami (similar to origami, except that cutting is allowed in addition to folding).
sentences.zh: 我们俩小时候都接触过折纸和剪纸（与折纸类似，只不过除了折叠之外还可以切割）。

sentences.en: We, along with our colleagues, decided to see if those traditional techniques could be used on the new material to eliminate the need for molds.
sentences.zh: 我们和我们的同事决定看看这些传统技术是否可以用于新材料以消除对模具的需求。

sentences.en: Our goal was to allow crafters to make hybrid papercraft creations that contained easily integrated elements such as LEDs and motors.
sentences.zh: 我们的目标是让工匠们能够制作出包含 LED 和电机等易于集成元件的混合纸工艺品。

blocks.html_en: In particular, we were interested in the possibility of combining the separate stages of creating a papercraft object and adding electrical conductors. Previous approaches to creating electrified papercraft objects relied on adding a separate flexible conductor—such as adhesive copper tape—to the paper. This increases the effort required and runs the risk of creating open circuits as the conductive material conforms to the object’s shape.

sentences.en: In particular, we were interested in the possibility of combining the separate stages of creating a papercraft object and adding electrical conductors.
sentences.zh: 特别是，我们对将创建纸制品和添加电导体的各个阶段结合起来的可能性感兴趣。

sentences.en: Previous approaches to creating electrified papercraft objects relied on adding a separate flexible conductor—such as adhesive copper tape—to the paper.
sentences.zh: 以前创建带电纸工艺品的方法依赖于在纸张上添加单独的柔性导体（例如铜胶带）。

sentences.en: This increases the effort required and runs the risk of creating open circuits as the conductive material conforms to the object’s shape.
sentences.zh: 这增加了所需的工作量，并且由于导电材料符合物体的形状，因此存在产生开路的风险。

sentences.en: Isopropanol and a gallium-indium liquid material are used to impregnate a paperlike material that is 55 percent polyester and 45 percent cellulose.
sentences.zh: 使用异丙醇和镓铟液体材料来浸渍由 55% 聚酯和 45% 纤维素组成的纸状材料。

sentences.en: Electronic components such as LEDs and motors are held in place with masking tape.
sentences.zh: LED 和电机等电子元件用遮蔽胶带固定到位。

sentences.en: James Provost
sentences.zh: 詹姆斯·普罗沃斯特

sentences.zh: 我们很快证实，折叠和切割所涉及的压力（范围从 2.5 到 100 兆帕）足以形成导电迹线。

sentences.en: We also confirmed that normal handling of the paper didn’t accidentally create conductive paths.
sentences.zh: 我们还确认，正常处理纸张不会意外地产生导电路径。

blocks.html_en: We made a number of changes to the original method for creating the impregnated paper. For example, instead of immersing the paper in a mixture of isopropanol and liquid metal, we used an airbrush to spray the mixture onto the paper. That allowed us to vary how much was deposited on the paper and to use cardboard stencils to mask some areas from being impregnated, allowing folding and cutting in those regions without creating unwanted conductive traces. We also experimented with the ratios of isopropanol and liquid metal.

sentences.en: We made a number of changes to the original method for creating the impregnated paper.
sentences.zh: 我们对制造浸渍纸的原始方法进行了一些更改。

sentences.en: For example, instead of immersing the paper in a mixture of isopropanol and liquid metal, we used an airbrush to spray the mixture onto the paper.
sentences.zh: 例如，我们没有将纸浸入异丙醇和液态金属的混合物中，而是使用喷枪将混合物喷到纸上。

sentences.en: That allowed us to vary how much was deposited on the paper and to use cardboard stencils to mask some areas from being impregnated, allowing folding and cutting in those regions without creating unwanted conductive traces.
sentences.zh: 这使我们能够改变纸张上沉积的量，并使用纸板模板来掩盖某些区域免遭浸渍，从而允许在这些区域进行折叠和切割，而不会产生不需要的导电痕迹。

sentences.en: We also experimented with the ratios of isopropanol and liquid metal.
sentences.zh: 我们还对异丙醇和液态金属的比例进行了实验。

sentences.en: We became frequent visitors to our university’s materials science and engineering department.
sentences.zh: 我们成为我们大学材料科学与工程系的常客。

sentences.en: After optimizing the mixing ratios and amount applied via airbrush, we were left with a material that reliably conducts with a resistance of 23.18 ohms per centimeter for cut edges and 4.4 Ω/cm for folded edges.
sentences.zh: 在优化混合比例和通过喷枪涂抹的量后，我们得到了一种可靠导电的材料，切割边缘的电阻为每厘米 23.18 欧姆，折叠边缘的电阻为 4.4 Ω/cm。

sentences.en: The folded edges retain their conductivity even if later flattened out, and the conductivity is the same on either side of the paper.
sentences.zh: 即使后来变平，折叠边缘仍能保持其导电性，并且纸张两侧的导电性相同。

sentences.en: We estimate the combined cost of the paper and liquid metal (available from many online vendors) is about US $1.80 to make a 10- by 10-cm piece.
sentences.zh: 我们估计，制作 10 x 10 厘米的纸张和液态金属（可从许多在线供应商处购买）的总成本约为 1.80 美元。

sentences.zh: 下一步是将电子元件连接到迹线上。

sentences.en: To make the connections more flexible, we cut down the rigid leads of LEDs and attached conductive thread to the stumps.
sentences.zh: 为了使连接更加灵活，我们切断了 LED 的刚性引线，并将导电线连接到树桩上。

sentences.en: We then held the threads in place using masking tape.
sentences.zh: 然后我们使用遮蔽胶带将螺纹固定到位。

sentences.en: Similarly, we connected conductive thread to the terminals of a power supply.
sentences.zh: 同样，我们将导电线连接到电源的端子上。

sentences.en: The first is based on Chinese papercraft in which a piece of paper is folded into a fanlike segment and then cut to create a radially symmetric design.

sentences.en: We provided circles of paper with a doughnot-shape impregnated region, with an untreated region that created a gap in the donut.

sentences.en: We attached positive and negative terminals to either side of the gap.
sentences.zh: 我们将正极和负极端子连接到间隙的两侧。

sentences.en: The user could specify in the software how many times they wanted to fold the disk and then draw potential cuts, receiving immediate feedback on what the unfolded disk would look like, as well as guidance on how to place LEDs.

sentences.en: To make our paper sample, isopropanol and liquid metal are mixed in specific ratios while being cooled by an ice bath.

sentences.en: Sonic waves are used to ensure the liquid metal breaks up into microscopic droplets.
sentences.zh: 声波用于确保液态金属分解成微小的液滴。

sentences.en: The mixture is then applied via airbrush, while stencils prevent some areas being covered for different papercraft templates.
sentences.zh: 然后通过喷枪涂抹混合物，同时模板可以防止某些区域被不同的纸模模板覆盖。

sentences.en: The software tool provided templates for 12 origami designs, with step-by-step instructions for folding them.

sentences.zh: 项目完成后，用户可以通过将电机等组件粘贴到折叠处来添加组件。

blocks.html_en: The final version supported 3D paper model making. In this case, the initial paper supplied was a rectangle with an untreated rectangular central area. By cutting this paper in half and then further cutting the halves into patterns separated by a spacer, the user could make various self-standing models. The software allowed the user to draw a pattern on screen, and then have a cutting machine produce a template for cutting the impregnated paper.

sentences.en: The final version supported 3D paper model making.
sentences.zh: 最终版本支持3D纸模型制作。

sentences.en: In this case, the initial paper supplied was a rectangle with an untreated rectangular central area.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，最初提供的纸张是具有未经处理的矩形中心区域的矩形。

sentences.en: By cutting this paper in half and then further cutting the halves into patterns separated by a spacer, the user could make various self-standing models.
sentences.zh: 通过将这张纸切成两半，然后将两半进一步切成由垫片分隔开的图案，用户可以制作各种自立模型。

sentences.en: The software allowed the user to draw a pattern on screen, and then have a cutting machine produce a template for cutting the impregnated paper.
sentences.zh: 该软件允许用户在屏幕上绘制图案，然后让切割机生成用于切割浸渍纸的模板。

blocks.html_en: We had 42 participants, evenly divided into three groups, try out the different versions. All found it easy to use, and we were pleasantly surprised that some participants moved beyond the supplied designs to their own creations.

sentences.en: We had 42 participants, evenly divided into three groups, try out the different versions.
sentences.zh: 我们有 42 名参与者，平均分为三组，尝试不同的版本。

sentences.en: All found it easy to use, and we were pleasantly surprised that some participants moved beyond the supplied designs to their own creations.
sentences.zh: 所有人都发现它很容易使用，我们感到惊喜的是，一些参与者超越了提供的设计，转向了自己的创作。

blocks.html_en: For full details of the current process, see our open access LiqMetCraft research paper published in CHI ‘26: Proceedings of the 2026 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems. In the future, we plan to try different substrates for the impregnating solution, as well as explore further types of papercraft, such as pop-up books. We’re also interested in developing ways to use the material to support inputs as well as outputs by constructing switches and potentiometers directly out of the material. Imagine traditional papercraft creations becoming interactive devices!

sentences.en: For full details of the current process, see our open access LiqMetCraft research paper published in CHI ‘26: Proceedings of the 2026 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems.
sentences.zh: 有关当前流程的完整详细信息，请参阅我们在 CHI ‘26：2026 年 CHI 计算系统人为因素会议上发表的开放获取 LiqMetCraft 研究论文。

sentences.en: In the future, we plan to try different substrates for the impregnating solution, as well as explore further types of papercraft, such as pop-up books.
sentences.zh: 未来，我们计划尝试不同的基材用于浸渍溶液，并探索更多类型的纸工艺，例如立体书。

sentences.en: We’re also interested in developing ways to use the material to support inputs as well as outputs by constructing switches and potentiometers directly out of the material.
sentences.zh: 我们还对开发使用该材料来支持输入和输出的方法感兴趣，方法是直接用该材料构建开关和电位器。

sentences.en: Imagine traditional papercraft creations becoming interactive devices!
sentences.zh: 想象一下传统的纸艺作品变成了互动设备！

blocks.html_en: This article appears in the July 2026 print issue.

sentences.en: This article appears in the July 2026 print issue.
sentences.zh: 本文发表于 2026 年 7 月印刷版。

content_html_zh:
如果只需弯曲一张纸就能做出电路走线，你会做什么？ 如何将现代技术和传统手工艺联系起来，同时提供学习这两种技能的机会？

作为香港城市大学创意媒体学院 MEI 实验室数字工艺跨学科研究的一部分，我们发现了一项研究，展示了如何用用于制造导电墨水的液态金属浸渍纸状材料（技术上是“无纺布”）。 最初，浸渍材料是不导电的，因为形成了绝缘氧化物层，封装了液态金属的微小液滴。 然而，通过成型模具施加压力会使绝缘层破裂，使相邻颗粒合并，从而在模具形状中形成导电区域。

我们俩小时候都接触过折纸和剪纸（与折纸类似，只不过除了折叠之外还可以切割）。 我们和我们的同事决定看看这些传统技术是否可以用于新材料以消除对模具的需求。 我们的目标是让工匠们能够制作出包含 LED 和电机等易于集成元件的混合纸工艺品。

特别是，我们对将创建纸制品和添加电导体的各个阶段结合起来的可能性感兴趣。 以前创建带电纸工艺品的方法依赖于在纸张上添加单独的柔性导体（例如铜胶带）。 这增加了所需的工作量，并且由于导电材料符合物体的形状，因此存在产生开路的风险。

使用异丙醇和镓铟液体材料来浸渍由 55% 聚酯和 45% 纤维素组成的纸状材料。 LED 和电机等电子元件用遮蔽胶带固定到位。 詹姆斯·普罗沃斯特

我们的第一步是看看仅弯曲和切割所涉及的压力是否足以形成导电迹线。 我们成为我们大学材料科学与工程系的常客，制作样品，然后借用设备来表征它们的行为。

我们很快证实，折叠和切割所涉及的压力（范围从 2.5 到 100 兆帕）足以形成导电迹线。 我们还确认，正常处理纸张不会意外地产生导电路径。

我们对制造浸渍纸的原始方法进行了一些更改。 例如，我们没有将纸浸入异丙醇和液态金属的混合物中，而是使用喷枪将混合物喷到纸上。 这使我们能够改变纸张上沉积的量，并使用纸板模板来掩盖某些区域免遭浸渍，从而允许在这些区域进行折叠和切割，而不会产生不需要的导电痕迹。 我们还对异丙醇和液态金属的比例进行了实验。

我们成为我们大学材料科学与工程系的常客。

在优化混合比例和通过喷枪涂抹的量后，我们得到了一种可靠导电的材料，切割边缘的电阻为每厘米 23.18 欧姆，折叠边缘的电阻为 4.4 Ω/cm。 即使后来变平，折叠边缘仍能保持其导电性，并且纸张两侧的导电性相同。 我们估计，制作 10 x 10 厘米的纸张和液态金属（可从许多在线供应商处购买）的总成本约为 1.80 美元。

下一步是将电子元件连接到迹线上。 为了使连接更加灵活，我们切断了 LED 的刚性引线，并将导电线连接到树桩上。 然后我们使用遮蔽胶带将螺纹固定到位。 同样，我们将导电线连接到电源的端子上。

由于我们的目标是在教育上使用这种材料，因此我们现在需要让初学者（无论是纸艺还是电子学）能够轻松尝试。 我们创建了一个名为 LiqMetCraft 的工具包。 这包括所有必需的材料，加上一个基于浏览器的软件工具，可以让用户选择或创建设计，然后提供物理构造的指导。

我们创建了 LiqMetCraft 的三个版本。 第一种是基于中国的纸艺，将一张纸折叠成扇形，然后切割以形成径向对称的设计。 我们提供了带有甜甜圈形状浸渍区域的纸圈，以及未处理的区域，在甜甜圈中形成了间隙。 我们将正极和负极端子连接到间隙的两侧。 用户可以在软件中指定他们想要折叠圆盘多少次，然后绘制可能的切口，立即接收有关展开圆盘外观的反馈，以及如何放置 LED 的指导。

为了制作我们的纸样，异丙醇和液态金属以特定比例混合，同时通过冰浴冷却。 声波用于确保液态金属分解成微小的液滴。 然后通过喷枪涂抹混合物，同时模板可以防止某些区域被不同的纸模模板覆盖。 詹姆斯·普罗沃斯特

LiqMetCraft 的第二个版本基于折纸。 我们提供了矩形纸片，其中有两个导电区域，中间由边框隔开。 该软件工具提供了 12 种折纸设计的模板，以及折叠它们的分步说明。 项目完成后，用户可以通过将电机等组件粘贴到折叠处来添加组件。

最终版本支持3D纸模型制作。 在这种情况下，最初提供的纸张是具有未经处理的矩形中心区域的矩形。 通过将这张纸切成两半，然后将两半进一步切成由垫片分隔开的图案，用户可以制作各种自立模型。 该软件允许用户在屏幕上绘制图案，然后让切割机生成用于切割浸渍纸的模板。

我们有 42 名参与者，平均分为三组，尝试不同的版本。 所有人都发现它很容易使用，我们感到惊喜的是，一些参与者超越了提供的设计，转向了自己的创作。

有关当前流程的完整详细信息，请参阅我们在 CHI ‘26：2026 年 CHI 计算系统人为因素会议上发表的开放获取 LiqMetCraft 研究论文。 未来，我们计划尝试不同的基材用于浸渍溶液，并探索更多类型的纸工艺，例如立体书。 我们还对开发使用该材料来支持输入和输出的方法感兴趣，方法是直接用该材料构建开关和电位器。 想象一下传统的纸艺作品变成了互动设备！

本文发表于 2026 年 7 月印刷版。
